mnemonics: ACF: Always Check For correlation. PACF: Please Auto-Correct For (partial) correlation after removing others.
ARMA: Auto-Regressive (uses past values) and Moving Average (uses past errors) together.
keyPoints: A stationary process has a constant mean, constant variance, and an autocovariance that depends only on the lag, not on absolute time.

The Autocorrelation Function (ACF) measures the correlation between observations at different time lags. The Partial Autocorrelation Function (PACF) measures the correlation between observations at two points in time, with the linear effect of the intermediate observations removed.
An AR(p) model uses past p values to predict the present. An MA(q) model uses past q error terms to predict the present. An ARMA(p,q) model combines both.
commonMistakes: Assuming a series is stationary without performing statistical tests (e.g., ADF test) or visually inspecting the plot.
Confusing the theoretical ACF/PACF patterns of AR, MA, and ARMA models, leading to incorrect model identification.
Overfitting the model by choosing values for p and q that are too high, which captures noise instead of the underlying signal.
